name: GLINA 32
tagline: eight hands on the wheel, one morning, one clay, one fire.
equipment: four Shimpo VL-Whisper electric pottery wheels (one per student)
glaze lab: 12 dip-buckets, 3 brush jars, 1 wax-resist table
drying shelf — 3 tiers, 24 boards, slow-vent cover
apron rack — 8 linen aprons, olive-moss dye
central chalkboard with the morning's clay body, firing schedule, and one word
long pine bench for 8 with brass hooks under edge
audience: Zagreb locals who want one evening off screens
tourists from Vienna + Ljubljana on weekend trips
architects and designers wanting material intuition
rituals: guest arrives 09:45 → ties on linen apron with initials → host writes 'the word of the day' on chalkboard → 10:00 wedging clay by hand on oak table → 10:30 each pair at a wheel, 50-min throw → noon break, burek + kava → 13:00 trim + sign underside → 14:30 fire in kiln → return after 4 days for glazed piece
signature_session: MORNING 8 — eight students, one 2kg ball each, three forms to choose (cup · bowl · vase), three glazes (olive · bone · adriatic), one signature on underside, one shared photo at the oak table before firing
unique: the only pottery class in Zagreb where students write their own word on the morning chalkboard, throw on Shimpo Whisper wheels in pin-drop silence, and leave with a signed, photographed, fired piece — glazed in one of three house colours only
materials: waxed oak wedging table · brass hooks · canvas · linen aprons (olive-moss) · pine floorboards · lime-washed walls · terracotta bisque shelves · adriatic-steel kiln · hand-thrown chalk cup on the board rail
brandmark: numeral 32 in deep olive-moss inside a hand-drawn thrown circle · 'GLINA 32 · Zagreb' set in GT Sectra small caps under it · stamped on kraft clay-tags tied with twine to each fired piece
